result.stdout:
Usage: datask-excel [command] [option]

Commands
    excel      Commands to create, validate and update Excel files from a database.
    generate   Commands related to generating output from the Excel files.
    populate   Populates an empty database with data from an Excel workbook.

commands.payload:
Usage: datask-excel [command] [option]

Commands
    excel      Commands to create, validate and update Excel files from a database.
    generate   Commands related to generating output from the Excel files.
    populate   Populates an empty database with data from an Excel workbook.

result.stdout:
Usage: datask-excel excel [command] [option]

Commands
    create     Creates an Excel workbook from the schema of a database.
    details    <No description provided>
    validate   Validates an Excel workbook against a database

commands.payload:
Usage: datask-excel excel [command] [option]

Commands
    create     Creates an Excel workbook from the schema of a database.
    details    <No description provided>
    validate   Validates an Excel workbook against a database

result.stdout:
Usage: datask-excel excel create [arguments] [options]

Arguments
    excel file          The path to the Excel workbook to create.
    connection string   The connection string to the database to create the Excel file from.

Options
    --include, -i   Tables to include in generation; should match the format <schema>.<table>. Use regular express syntax.
    --exclude, -e   Tables to exclude in generation; should match the format <schema>.<table>. Use regular express syntax.
    --populate      Populates the Excel workbook with data from the database.
    --force         Overwrites the output file, if it already exists.

commands.payload:
Usage: datask-excel excel create [arguments] [options]

Arguments
    excel file          The path to the Excel workbook to create.
    connection string   The connection string to the database to create the Excel file from.

Options
    --include, -i   Tables to include in generation; should match the format <schema>.<table>. Use regular express syntax.
    --exclude, -e   Tables to exclude in generation; should match the format <schema>.<table>. Use regular express syntax.
    --populate      Populates the Excel workbook with data from the database.
    --force         Overwrites the output file, if it already exists.

result.stdout:
Usage: datask-excel excel validate [arguments] [options]

Arguments
    excel file          The path to the Excel workbook to create.
    connection string   The connection string to the database to create the Excel file from.

Options
    --fix   Start an interactive session to fix validation issues.

result.stdout:
Usage: datask-excel generate [command] [option]

Commands
    extensions, e   Generates extension methods.
    scripts         Generates SQL INSERT scripts from the Excel workbook.

commands.payload:
Usage: datask-excel generate [command] [option]

Commands
    extensions, e   Generates extension methods.
    scripts         Generates SQL INSERT scripts from the Excel workbook.

result.stdout:

result.stdout:
Usage: datask-excel generate scripts [arguments] [option]

Arguments
    excel file   The path to the Excel workbook to generate from.
    output       The path to the scripts file to create.

commands.payload:

Arguments

result.stdout:
Usage: datask-excel populate [arguments] [options]

Arguments
    excel file          The path to the Excel workbook to create.
    connection string   The connection string to the database to create the Excel file from.

Options
    --validate   Validates the Excel workbook against the database schema before attempting to populate.

commands.payload:

Options
    --validate   Validates the Excel workbook against the database schema before attempting to populate.
